name: Souffleed Acorn Squash
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Wash the outside of the squash well to remove any grit.
3. Using a sharp knife, cut a very thin slice off both ends of the squash so that each half remains stable on a plate and doesn't wobble.
4. Cut the squash in half and scoop out the seeds with a spoon; discard them.
5. Place the halved squash, skin side up, in a baking dish filled with about 1/2 inch of water so the flesh steams and stays moist while it bakes for 30 minutes.
6. Remove the baking dish from the oven. Turn each squash half cut side up using tongs; place 1 tablespoon of butter in each half. Return the squash to the oven for another 30 minutes or until tender.
7. Using tongs, carefully remove each squash half and gently pour the melted butter into a mixing bowl.
8. Carefully spoon out the cooked squash from each half into a bowl, leaving some flesh around the cavity; do not pierce the skin.
9. In a blender or food processor puree the squash (in two batches if necessary) with reserved melted butter, sugar, 1/4 teaspoon salt, 1/4 teaspoon pepper, and egg yolk. Pour this into the mixing bowl.
10. In another bowl, whip the egg white with a pinch of salt until stiff; fold it into the squash mixture.
11. Pour equal amounts of the soufflé mixture into each cavity and bake on a baking sheet for 25 minutes or until the tops are brown and begin to crack.
12. Serve in the squash shells as an appetizer or side dish.
